title: Decadent Dutch Epoisses Gratin
description: A peppery, molten, comforting Dutch dish featuring Epoisses cheese, this Jain-friendly gratin is perfect for a cold winter's night. The nimono-simmer method adds a depth of flavor to the dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, combine the sliced onions and minced garlic and cook over medium heat until the onions are translucent.
Add the sliced potatoes, vegetable broth, black pepper, and nutmeg to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the potato mixture for 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are slightly tender.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, arrange a layer of the potato mixture.
Sprinkle a third of the crumbled Epoisses cheese over the potato layer.
Repeat the layers two more times, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Dot the top of the cheese layer with the unsalted butter.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the gratin and serve hot.
